chapter_title.en: Chapter 73 – Selling of the fruits and its buying

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/176)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/176)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَنْ بَاعَ نَخْلًا قَدْ لَقِحَ فَالثَّمَرَةُ لِلْبَائِعِ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشْتَرِطَ الْمُبْتَاعُ قَضَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) بِذَلِكَ.

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban Bin Usman, from Yahya Bin Abu Al A’ala who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The one who sells a palm tree which had pollinated, so the fruits are for the seller, except if the buyer had stipulated otherwise. Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> had judged with that’.
en.sarwar: 12. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah from more than one person from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Yahya ibn abu al-‘Ala’ who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one sells palm trees which are pollinated, the fruit then belongs to the seller unless the buyer has stipulated otherwise. This is how the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, judged a case.’”
